assessment.body: aider is an interactive TUI/REPL that reads typed input from its controlling terminal, so it can receive backchannel-injected text and an interrupt. The adapter declares Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC} (core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go) and the shared control permission (agent.ControlPermission). The daemon writes back through whichever terminal backend owns the session's pty, resolved from session.Launcher: tmux send-keys / kitty send-text daemon-side, iTerm2/Terminal.app via the macOS app. InputService gates every write (backchannel toggle -> control consent -> controllable). Remotely the same path is reached via the relay control frame, double-gated by the relay-control toggle. Verified end-to-end against a real tmux pane by TestBackchannelE2E_Local/_Remote.

sources.note: tmux/kitty injectors + AppleScript broadcast keyed off Launcher
